notes: Wikipedia stored_url resolves to the same entity (South African pianist, b. 1934). birthYear 1934, instrument piano, birth name Adolph Johannes Brand, formerly Dollar Brand, and 'Mannenberg' as anti-apartheid anthem all match the extract verbatim. Editorial superlative ('most important South African jazz musician') not flagged.

Stored Wikipedia URL was a disambiguation page, but ours.wikipediaUrl points to Andrew_Hill_(jazz_musician), which websearch (resolved=true) confirms is the correct entity. birthYear 1931, deathYear 2007, piano+composer, and Blue Note avant-garde / 'Point of Departure' all confirmed by websearch findings/note.

Wikipedia entity-matches (jazz singer/pianist, 1939-2025). birthYear 1939 (Oct 28 1939), deathYear 2025 (Apr 26 2025), vocals+piano, and late-career HighNote recordings (The World According to Andy Bey 2013, Pages from an Imaginary Life 2014, both HighNote) all match. 'Baritone voice' matches 'four-octave baritone voice.'

Wikipedia entity-matches (Hammond organist). birthYear 1933 matches the primary date Wikipedia gives ('1933 according to researchers Bob Eagle and Eric LeBlanc, though other sources state 1934 or 1937'). deathYear 1971 (Apr 1 1971), organ, gospel roots, switch to organ around 1958, two Blue Note leader albums Face to Face and Stop and Listen (both 1961), return to Chicago and trio in 1963, two more Argo albums all match the extract/discography.

Wikipedia entity-matches (South African jazz musician, 1955-2008). birthYear 1955, deathYear 2008, piano+saxophone, 1991 'Celebration' on World Circuit with Courtney Pine and Mercury Music Prize nomination, and Verve signing with first album featuring Joe Henderson/Abbey Lincoln/Elvin Jones all match the extract verbatim.
Bio is essentially verbatim from the Wikipedia lead. birthYear 1960 (Aug 26 1960), saxophone, Branford Marsalis Quartet, Buckshot LeFonque, and Tonight Show Band 1992-1995 all match.

Stored Wikipedia URL was a disambiguation page; ours.wikipediaUrl points to Dave_Holland_(bassist), confirmed correct by websearch (resolved=true). birthYear 1946, living (no death), bass, Miles Davis 'Bitches Brew', and ECM recordings all confirmed by websearch note. 'Joined Davis at 22' consistent with b.1946 and Bitches Brew (1969).

Stored Wikipedia URL (_(musician)) returned no page; ours.wikipediaUrl points to David_Murray_(saxophonist), confirmed correct by websearch (resolved=true). birthYear 1955 (Feb 19 1955), living, tenor sax + bass clarinet, and co-founding the World Saxophone Quartet all confirmed by websearch note. 'Over 100 albums' and loft-jazz scene not contradicted.

Wikipedia entity-matches. birthYear 1917, deathYear 1993, trumpet, bebop co-founding with Charlie Parker, bent horn/puffed cheeks, and the 'A Night in Tunisia, written in 1942 while with Earl Hines's band... syncopated bass line' claim all match the extract (the last nearly verbatim). The Chano Pozo / 'Manteca' Afro-Cuban collaboration is not in the (truncated) extract but is not contradicted; entity is unambiguous and core specific claims match, so not flagged.

Wikipedia entity-matches (musician of Puerto Rican heritage, 1936-2025). birthYear 1936 (Dec 15 1936), deathYear 2025 (Aug 6 2025), piano, influence of Monk and McCoy Tyner, nine Grammy Awards (most recently 2006 'Simpático'), and founder of Harlem River Drive all match the extract verbatim.

Wikipedia entity-matches (Brazilian jazz singer, b.1942). birthYear 1942 (Mar 6 1942), living, vocals, six-octave range, Return to Forever and Airto Moreira, and 'Butterfly Dreams' (1973) all match. The other named album 'Open Your Eyes, You Can Fly' is not listed in the extract but is consistent with the documented string of 1970s Milestone albums and is not contradicted.

Wikipedia entity-matches (jazz trombonist, 1937-2022). birthYear 1937 (Jun 3 1937), deathYear 2022 (Jun 3 2022), trombone, and adventurous Blue Note compositions (Evolution 1963, Some Other Stuff 1964; bulk of compositions on McLean's One Step Beyond / Destination Out) all match. Remaining content is editorial.

Wikipedia entity-matches (Brazilian multi-instrumentalist, 1936-2025). birthYear 1936 (Jun 22 1936), deathYear 2025 (Sep 13 2025), and instruments piano/flute/saxophone/accordion/guitar all match. 'O Bruxo' rendered as 'The Wizard' vs Wikipedia's 'the Sorcerer' is the same Portuguese term glossed differently (not an error). Teapots/animals/kitchen objects match 'teapots, children's toys, and animals.' The 'Slaves Mass' album description is not in the extract but is not contradicted.

fieldIssues.evidenceQuote: Willie James "Blood" Ulmer (February 8, 1940 – June 3, 2026) was an American jazz, free funk and blues guitarist and singer.

notes: Right entity (Wikipedia confirms guitarist/singer, Ornette Coleman's harmolodics, 'Tales of Captain Black' 1979, 'Are You Glad to Be in America?' 1980). deathYear 2026 matches Wikipedia (June 3, 2026). But the birthYear field is 1942 while Wikipedia gives Feb 8, 1940 and short description '(1940-2026)' — a clear field contradiction. Bio prose itself is clean; only the birthYear field is wrong.

No English Wikipedia article exists (stored en URL is a 404). ours.wikipediaUrl points to ja.wikipedia (稲垣次郎), confirmed correct by websearch (resolved=true; ja/de Wikipedia + Wikidata Q105576139). birthYear 1933 (Oct 3 1933), deathYear 2024 (Jan 18 2024), tenor/soprano sax + flute, and leadership of Soul Media all confirmed. The websearch note's 'ours had 2016, WRONG' refers to the pre-repair value; the current deathYear 2024 is correct. 'Funky Stuff' as a jazz-funk holy grail is editorial.

Stored Wikipedia URL (_(musician)) is a 404; ours.wikipediaUrl points to Leon_Thomas_(jazz_singer), confirmed correct by websearch (resolved=true). birthYear 1937 (Oct 4 1937), deathYear 1999 (May 8 1999), vocals, yodeling/glottal-stop style compared to African Pygmy singing, and 'Karma' with Pharoah Sanders all confirmed by websearch note.
Bio is essentially verbatim from the Wikipedia lead. birthYear 1909, deathYear 1959 (Aug 27 1909 - Mar 15 1959), tenor saxophone + clarinet, and nicknames 'Pres'/'Prez' all match.

Wikipedia entity-matches (South African jazz drummer, 1940-2025; resolved title 'Louis Moholo'). birthYear 1940, deathYear 2025 (Mar 10 1940 - Jun 13 2025), drums+percussion, founding the Blue Notes with Chris McGregor and Johnny Dyani, 1964 emigration and London settlement, Brotherhood of Breath, Cecil Taylor, and death in Cape Town on 13 June 2025 all match the extract.

Wikipedia entity-matches (jazz saxophonist/clarinetist, b.1975). birthYear 1975, living, alto saxophone (confirmed by 'Rising Star Alto Saxophone'), composer, AACM membership, and the multichapter 'Coin Coin' series exploring history/memory/ancestry all match. The 'vocals' instrument is consistent with Coin Coin's spoken-word/voice element and is not contradicted.

Bio is verbatim from the Wikipedia lead. birthYear 1949, deathYear 2007 (Mar 29 1949 - Jan 13 2007), saxophone, 900+ albums, 15 Grammys, DownBeat Hall of Fame 2007, Berklee honorary doctorate 2004, and death from leukemia/MDS all match.

Bio is verbatim from the Wikipedia lead. birthYear 1923, deathYear 1999 (Jan 1 1923 - Oct 9 1999), vibraphone, nickname 'Bags', Modern Jazz Quartet membership, and hard bop/post-bop collaborations all match.

Wikipedia entity-matches. birthYear 1930, deathYear 2015 (Mar 9 1930 - Jun 11 2015), alto sax/violin/trumpet, 1959 Atlantic 'The Shape of Jazz to Come', 1960 'Free Jazz: A Collective Improvisation' naming the movement, plastic (Grafton) saxophone, harmolodics, and Prime Time electric band all match the extract.

Wikipedia entity-matches (jazz guitarist, 1944-2021). birthYear 1944 (Aug 25 1944), deathYear 2021 (Nov 1 2021), guitar, and the 1980 hemorrhaged arteriovenous malformation / brain surgery / memory loss / relearning the instrument all match the extract closely.

fieldIssues.evidenceQuote: The group was active from 1970–1977, and reunited briefly in 2004.

notes: Right entity (free jazz trio: Leroy Jenkins violin, Sirone bass, Jerome Cooper drums/piano — all match, as do loft-jazz/no-chordal-instrument/cooperative claims and the instruments field piano/drums/bass/violin). The group-formation field 'birthYear' is 1971, but Wikipedia states the group was 'active from 1970–1977' and refers to 'the formation of the Revolutionary Ensemble in 1970.' One-year discrepancy; bio prose ('active primarily in the 1970s') is fine. Lower confidence because '1970' could be read as a coalescence period, but the lead's explicit formation year is 1970.

Wikipedia entity-matches (funk/soul/jazz, 1940-2025). birthYear 1940 (Sep 10 1940), deathYear 2025 (Mar 4 2025), vibraphone, Roy Ayers Ubiquity, jazz-funk pioneering, heavily sampled by rappers, 'Everybody Loves the Sunshine' and 'Running Away', performing live until 2023, and death at age 84 in March 2025 all match the extract.
